title: Red Hat Security Advisory: expat security update
overview:
An update for expat is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.6 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: expat security update

An update for expat is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.6 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for expat is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.6 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
expat-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.src.rpm
expat-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.i686.rpm
expat-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.x86_64.rpm
expat-debuginfo-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.i686.rpm
expat-debuginfo-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.x86_64.rpm
expat-debugsource-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.i686.rpm
expat-debugsource-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.x86_64.rpm
expat-devel-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.i686.rpm
expat-devel-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.x86_64.rpm
expat-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.s390x.rpm
expat-debuginfo-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.s390x.rpm
expat-debugsource-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.s390x.rpm
expat-devel-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.s390x.rpm
expat-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.ppc64le.rpm
expat-debuginfo-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.ppc64le.rpm
expat-debugsource-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.ppc64le.rpm
expat-devel-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.ppc64le.rpm
expat-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.aarch64.rpm
expat-debuginfo-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.aarch64.rpm
expat-debugsource-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.aarch64.rpm
expat-devel-2.5.0-5.el9_6.1.aarch64.rpm

CVE-2025-59375: A memory amplification vulnerability in libexpat allows attackers to trigger excessive dynamic memory allocations by submitting specially crafted XML input. A small input (~250 KiB) can cause the parser to allocate hundreds of megabytes, leading to denial-of-service (DoS) through memory exhaustion.
